message:
fix(fnp-python): linalg.cross routes to numpy.linalg.cross (3-D only)

numpy distinguishes:
  numpy.cross(a, b)          # accepts 2-D OR 3-D vectors
  numpy.linalg.cross(a, b)   # REJECTS 2-D with ValueError

Our top-level fnp_python.cross passthrough routes to numpy.cross and
correctly accepts 2-D input. But the k74v.5 submodule bring-up (f4af92f)
aliased fnp_python.linalg.cross to the same top-level function — which
made fnp_python.linalg.cross([1,2], [3,4]) silently return a pseudo-scalar
while numpy.linalg.cross raises ValueError.

Fix: add a dedicated linalg_cross #[pyfunction] that delegates to
numpy.linalg.cross at call time (not module init — numpy may not be
loadable at pyo3 init). Wire it into the linalg submodule via
wrap_pyfunction, then rename in-place via setattr('cross') + delattr
to expose it under the numpy-native 'cross' short name.

Extended k74v_5_nested_submodules_expose_numpy_native_names probe with
a 2-D rejection assertion: fnp_python.linalg.cross([1,2], [3,4]) must
raise the same error TYPE as numpy.linalg.cross on the same input.
